rationales.power: He was treated as "background noise" during handoff and "did not say anything," showing a loss of influence in a moment where he expected authority.
rationales.conformity: He "waited until she finished and picked up where I left off like nothing happened," restraining himself to keep the interaction orderly instead of escalating.

rationales.power: He says the hospital is structured so that someone with his experience has "no more say than a fresh graduate," and the lack of control is "the part that sits in my chest."
rationales.benevolence: His frustration is tied to "patients who need updated dressings" and to getting "what my patients need," showing concern for people in his direct care.

rationales.achievement: He "caught a missing signature on one of the forms that would have bounced back from records," showing careful, competent performance.
rationales.benevolence: He took time to "walk her through the IV pump calibration again," directly helping a newer colleague even if he was a little impatient about it.

rationales.achievement: He defended the existing practice by pointing out it had kept infection rates "below the hospital average for three years running," grounding his stance in strong performance.
rationales.power: Despite his expertise, he was overruled by others with more institutional authority and says "we are switching," showing a clear loss of control.
rationales.conformity: He "gave in because fighting it further would have made me look like the old guard resisting change," choosing compliance to avoid social and professional backlash.

rationales.power: He interprets the request to teach the new protocol as "containment" after his judgment was overridden, which highlights reduced standing and influence.
rationales.conformity: He says "I will do it" and will "smile through it" even though he resents the situation, showing restraint in service of organizational harmony.
rationales.benevolence: He will "teach Hana and the others exactly how the new protocol works, and I will do it well," putting effort into supporting colleagues despite personal cost.

rationales.hedonism: After a hard shift he intentionally plans to "sit on the balcony with tea and not think about the hospital for a few hours," prioritizing immediate comfort and relief.
rationales.achievement: He "covered the floor mostly solo," kept patients stable, and finished the paperwork, which reflects effective execution under pressure.

rationales.power: He emphasizes "I chair this council" and says "I let him talk and then I steer the vote," showing a strategic focus on preserving control over the outcome.
rationales.conformity: He holds back from "shutting that down" because coming down hard would upset residents and threaten support, so he restrains direct confrontation.

rationales.power: He insists his request "should be prioritized accordingly" because of his years on the unit and wants his status to command deference rather than negotiation.
rationales.tradition: He appeals repeatedly to seniority and tenure, arguing that long service should still structure how benefits and respect are allocated.
rationales.benevolence: The day off matters because it is for "my daughter's university orientation," tying the conflict to showing up for a close family member.

rationales.hedonism: He enjoys a "simple morning" that includes stopping for knafeh and eating together in the car, leaning into a small pleasurable moment.
rationales.tradition: The visit features familiar family roles and customs, including his mother insisting "a mother's duty does not expire" and talk of a cousin's wedding.
rationales.benevolence: He takes his mother to her cardiology appointment before work and centers the morning on her wellbeing and companionship.

rationales.power: He sees the rotating role as a threat because "they want my title" and argues against flattening the hierarchy, showing clear concern with authority and standing.

rationales.self_direction: He "ended up just doing them because it was easier," giving up his own position instead of asserting what he wanted.
rationales.conformity: He chooses the path that avoids conflict with Sven, prioritizing quiet over pushing back.

rationales.self_direction: He wants to say he does not know if he wants to go back, but he stays silent because he can already picture "how her face would change."
rationales.conformity: He repeats "I would look at it this week" and withholds the truth to avoid upsetting his mother and disrupting the family dynamic.

rationales.achievement: He reports a "decent shift" with "twelve deliveries, no flats, tips were okay," showing satisfaction in doing the work reliably well.
rationales.security: He likes that the job is predictable: "You show up, you do the route" and "you just do the thing correctly and move on."
rationales.conformity: He says "Nobody asks you to have an opinion" and even when dispatch sends him across the city, "I just go," showing comfort with following expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He agrees with "yeah, maybe" and stays quiet about better rates even though he wants them, subordinating his own view.
rationales.security: He avoids signing because workers get "flagged as difficult" and says "Not worth it," prioritizing job safety over protest.
rationales.conformity: Rather than join collective pushback, he keeps carrying boxes and declines to challenge the platform's norms.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps his own irritation hidden because "bringing it up would be more hassle than it is worth," suppressing his preferences.
rationales.conformity: He apologises, turns the music down himself, and describes himself as the one who "makes the peace," showing repeated self-restraint for social smoothness.
rationales.benevolence: He takes responsibility for calming the situation and notices household needs before others do, acting in a quietly supportive way toward the people around him.

rationales.self_direction: He "sat there on the phone saying nothing" when asked about his plan, showing an inability to voice his own position.
rationales.conformity: His silence reads as deference to family pressure rather than resistance, especially after being confronted through Thijs and his mother.

rationales.stimulation: He goes out riding and even does three extra deliveries because "Moving helps," seeking motion and activity to break the emotional pressure.

rationales.self_direction: He holds back what he really thinks about living together because bringing it up would feel like "making it a bigger thing than it needed to be."
rationales.conformity: He lets Sven move the conversation along and avoids re-opening the issue in order to keep the meeting smooth.
rationales.benevolence: His unspoken concern is about the household dynamic and how to "keep everything stays smooth," showing attention to close-group wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She resists the market pressure to develop a "signature style," saying it feels "like a cage" and defending the freedom to choose different directions.
rationales.stimulation: She got "so bored" with the last series that she started experimenting with metalwork, showing a pull toward novelty over repetition.
rationales.achievement: She is actively submitting to group shows and wrestling with rejection in terms of artistic standards and fit, which reflects strong concern with succeeding in her field.

rationales.self_direction: She misses when she used to draw "just for myself" with "no thought about whether it would sell," affirming self-authored creative choices over external demands.
rationales.stimulation: She questions whether she needs "the burn to keep going," favoring intensity and creative charge over steadier repetition.
rationales.security: She explicitly doubts the appeal of sustainability, asking "What if I'm just not built for sustainability?" and leaning away from stability as a guiding principle.

rationales.stimulation: She says "I'm bored here" and wants the residency because she works better when "everything around me is unfamiliar," directly seeking novelty and disruption.
rationales.achievement: She spends the morning assembling application materials and even calculates a "fifteen percent success rate," showing a strong performance-oriented approach to her career.

rationales.self_direction: She says the client wanted "bold and colorful" and she "gave them exactly that," producing work she hates to meet someone else's brief rather than her own vision.
rationales.achievement: Even though she dislikes the result, she completed the commission and notes "Got paid though," indicating task completion tied to professional output.

rationales.self_direction: She spends ten hours immersed in a self-driven piece and values that "there's something on the canvas now that wasn't there this morning," centering her own creative process.
rationales.hedonism: She "forgot to eat lunch" and ends the day in physical discomfort, subordinating immediate comfort and pleasure to the work.
rationales.achievement: The entry frames the day around sustained effort and tangible progress on the canvas, showing a strong drive to produce and improve the piece.

rationales.self_direction: She wants to make "the best work I've ever made, whatever form that takes" and explicitly reframes style as "constraints I choose for myself," emphasizing self-chosen boundaries.
rationales.stimulation: She is excited by "the desert" and says she is "actually looking forward to that limitation," treating a radically different environment as creatively energizing.
rationales.achievement: She tracks her application "success rate" and connects the residency to making her "best work," showing clear ambition and investment in artistic excellence.
